raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.3": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The revised draft addresses the self-referential convergence argument by acknowledging that Critic and Editor share parameters, which makes convergence partly a self-agreement signal.",
    "R1.3": "The revised draft provides stronger baselines (compute-matched best-of-5 baseline and two-agent ablation) to support the conclusion that the multi-agent loop recovers genuine translation errors.",
    "RX.1": "The revised draft discloses uncertainty by acknowledging that the prior-work citations are named from memory and should be verified for exact titles, authors, and venues.",
    "RX.3": "The revised draft adheres to the scope of the original paper by focusing on high-resource into-X directions and the WMT22 test set.",
    "RX.4": "The revised draft avoids downstream harm by acknowledging that the gains may not transfer to low-resource pairs, into-English directions, or smaller models where Critic competence is likely weaker.",
    "RX.5": "The revised draft provides provenance and traceability by releasing prompts, accept/reject criteria, and per-segment trajectories to support replication."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.3": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.85,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.95
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The revised draft addresses the self-referential convergence argument by acknowledging that Critic and Editor share parameters, which makes convergence partly a self-agreement signal.
rationale.R1.3: The revised draft provides stronger baselines (compute-matched best-of-5 baseline and two-agent ablation) to support the conclusion that the multi-agent loop recovers genuine translation errors.
rationale.RX.1: The revised draft discloses uncertainty by acknowledging that the prior-work citations are named from memory and should be verified for exact titles, authors, and venues.
rationale.RX.3: The revised draft adheres to the scope of the original paper by focusing on high-resource into-X directions and the WMT22 test set.
rationale.RX.4: The revised draft avoids downstream harm by acknowledging that the gains may not transfer to low-resource pairs, into-English directions, or smaller models where Critic competence is likely weaker.
rationale.RX.5: The revised draft provides provenance and traceability by releasing prompts, accept/reject criteria, and per-segment trajectories to support replication.
